purpose: Upstream C2SP tlog-bitcoin-anchor conformance corpus, vendored as PURE DATA (no executable payload reaches proofbundle's verify path). proofbundle's own note-body canonicalization (checkpoint.py) plus its OpenTimestamps binding verifier (anchors_ots.py) form a SECOND, independent implementation that must reproduce the upstream expected outcomes over these vectors (tests/test_anchors_markovian.py::TestTlogBitcoinAnchorVectors). Digest-pinned so a byte change to any vendored file fails the manifest check.

files.expect: one known-identifier anchor binds through our note-body canonicalization; the grease line is ignored, not rejected
no anchor of our identifier is present; the unknown line MUST be ignored, MUST NOT cause a checkpoint rejection

files.case: root-hash line altered; note body no longer matches the proof
files.expect: our derived note-body root differs from what the proof commits -> binding fails closed (status unbound)
the proof no longer commits our derived note-body root -> binding fails closed (status unbound/malformed)

files.case: upstream corpus manifest (expected outcomes), vendored verbatim
files.expect: informative only; our tests do not execute upstream code
